bogota.title: Bogotá
bogota.tagline: South America's Cultural Powerhouse at 2,640 Meters
bogota.gettingThere: Fly to El Dorado International Airport (BOG) — Colombia's main hub with direct flights from North America, Europe, and across Latin America. Uber or taxi to La Candelaria ~COP 25,000 ($6) in 30–60 min depending on traffic.

essentials.gettingAround: TransMilenio bus rapid transit covers the city. Uber and DiDi widely used. Taxis yellow and metered — insist on the meter. Altitude sickness possible on arrival; take it easy the first day.
trainConnections: Bus to Villa de Leyva (4 hr, ~COP 35,000)
essentials.language: Spanish, some English in upscale areas and hostels

medellin.title: Medellín
medellin.gettingThere: Fly to José María Córdova International Airport (MDE) in Rionegro — 45 min shuttle or taxi to the city (~COP 90,000 / $22). Domestic alternative: Enrique Olaya Herrera (EOH) in the city center for some routes.
highlights: Guatapé & Piedra del Peñol day trip

essentials.gettingAround: Metro is clean, cheap, and efficient — covers main corridors plus MetroCable gondolas to hillside neighborhoods. Uber and DiDi everywhere. Rental bikes via EnCicla (free public system).
essentials.language: Spanish, good English in El Poblado and Laureles

cartagena.title: Cartagena
cartagena.gettingThere: Fly to Rafael Núñez International Airport (CTG). Taxi or Uber to the Walled City ~COP 15,000 ($4) in 15 min. Direct flights from major US cities, Panama, and all Colombian cities.

essentials.gettingAround: Walled City and Getsemaní completely walkable. Uber for Bocagrande and further. Boats to Rosario Islands from the port. Be prepared for heat and humidity — carry water.
essentials.language: Spanish, decent English in tourist areas

santa-marta.title: Santa Marta
santa-marta.tagline: Where the Sierra Nevada Meets the Caribbean Sea
santa-marta.gettingThere: Fly to Simón Bolívar International Airport (SMR). Taxi to centro ~COP 12,000 ($3). Direct flights from Bogotá, Medellín, and Cali. Bus from Cartagena ~4 hours.

essentials.gettingAround: City center walkable. Colectivos and buses to Tayrona (~COP 7,000) and Minca. Uber limited — use local taxis. Book Ciudad Perdida trek through licensed agencies only (Wiwa Tours, Expotur, Magic Tour).
essentials.language: Spanish, limited English except at hostels

san-andres.title: San Andrés
san-andres.gettingThere: Fly to Gustavo Rojas Pinilla Airport (ADZ). Must purchase a tourist tax card (tarjeta de turismo, ~COP 130,000 / $32) on arrival. Direct flights from Bogotá, Medellín, Cali, and Cartagena.
highlights: Seafood at San Luis fishing village

essentials.gettingAround: Rent a golf cart (~COP 150,000/day) to circle the island. Motorbikes also popular. Taxis available. Boats to Johnny Cay and Aquarium from Spratt Bight dock.
essentials.language: Spanish, English Creole (local Raizal language), some English

cali.title: Cali
cali.gettingThere: Fly to Alfonso Bonilla Aragón International Airport (CLO) in Palmira — 30 min taxi to the city (~COP 60,000 / $15). Direct flights from Bogotá, Medellín, and some US cities.
highlights: Salsa dancing at Tin Tin Deo & Zaperoco

essentials.gettingAround: MIO bus system covers the city. Uber and DiDi available. Walkable in San Antonio and centro. Take salsa lessons — many schools in Barrio Obrero and El Peñón offer drop-in classes.

villa-de-leyva.title: Villa de Leyva
villa-de-leyva.gettingThere: No airport — reach by bus from Bogotá (4 hr, ~COP 35,000) or Tunja (45 min). Best as a weekend escape from Bogotá. Comfortable buses depart from Terminal Salitre.
highlights: Plaza Mayor — Colombia's largest cobblestone square

essentials.gettingAround: Completely walkable town center. Tuk-tuks and taxis for outlying sites. Rent a bicycle for surrounding countryside. Day trips to Ráquira and Ecce Homo by taxi or colectivo.
trainConnections: Bus to Barichara via Tunja & Bucaramanga (8 hr total)

salento.title: Salento
salento.tagline: Gateway to the Coffee Region & Cocora Valley
salento.gettingThere: No airport — bus from Armenia (45 min, ~COP 8,000) or Pereira (1.5 hr, ~COP 12,000). Fly into Armenia (AXM) or Pereira (PEI), then connect by bus. From Medellín by bus ~6 hours.
highlights: Valle de Cocora wax palm hike
Mirador Alto de la Cruz viewpoint
Artisan craft shops on the plaza

essentials.gettingAround: Town is tiny and walkable. Willy jeeps (Willys) to Cocora Valley from plaza (~COP 4,000 each way, 30 min). Shared jeeps depart when full. Boots recommended for Cocora Valley — trail gets muddy.

barichara.title: Barichara
barichara.gettingThere: No airport — reach by bus from Bucaramanga (3 hr, ~COP 25,000) via San Gil (45 min). Fly to Bucaramanga (BGA) then connect by bus. The road from San Gil is scenic and winding.
highlights: Camino Real stone path to Guane

essentials.gettingAround: Entirely walkable — bring comfortable shoes for cobblestones. Camino Real to Guane is 9 km one-way (2–3 hr hike). Taxi or moto-taxi back from Guane. San Gil nearby for adventure sports.
trainConnections: San Gil to Bogotá by bus (7 hr, ~COP 50,000)

leticia.title: Leticia
leticia.gettingThere: Fly to Alfredo Vásquez Cobo Airport (LET) — no roads connect Leticia to the rest of Colombia. Flights from Bogotá only (~2 hr). Twin city with Tabatinga, Brazil — walk across the border freely.

essentials.gettingAround: Town center walkable. River boats for all excursions — arrange through your lodge or local agencies. Book multi-day jungle stays in advance. Bring insect repellent, rain gear, and waterproof bags.
trainConnections: Walk to Tabatinga, Brazil (no border control)
essentials.language: Spanish, Portuguese in Tabatinga, indigenous languages, very limited English

tatacoa-desert.title: Tatacoa Desert
tatacoa-desert.gettingThere: No airport — bus from Neiva (45 min, ~COP 10,000) to Villavieja, then moto-taxi or colectivo into the desert. Fly to Neiva (NVA) from Bogotá or bus from Bogotá (5 hr, ~COP 40,000). Can combine with San Agustín on a southern loop.
highlights: Natural swimming pool in the desert
Valle de los Xilópalos fossil trail

essentials.gettingAround: Moto-taxi from Villavieja to the desert (~COP 15,000). Walking between El Cusco and Los Hoyos is possible but hot (7 km). Bring tons of water and sun protection. Best stargazing on clear nights — check moon phase.
trainConnections: Neiva to Bogotá by bus (5 hr, ~COP 40,000)
Neiva to San Agustín by bus (5 hr, ~COP 30,000)

providencia.title: Providencia
providencia.gettingThere: Fly from San Andrés to El Embrujo Airport (PVA) — 20-minute flight on small prop planes (Satena airline, ~COP 300,000 one-way). Alternatively, a catamaran runs from San Andrés (~3.5 hr, often cancelled due to rough seas). Book flights well in advance — limited seats.
highlights: World-class diving & sea of seven colors

essentials.gettingAround: Rent a scooter (~COP 80,000/day) or bicycle. Island is tiny — rideable in an hour. Small lanchas (boats) to Crab Cay and Santa Catalina. No Uber — use local moto-taxis. Cash preferred; limited ATMs.

travelTips.currency: Colombian Peso (COP). Cards widely accepted in cities and tourist areas. Always carry cash for small towns, colectivos, and street food. ATMs (Bancolombia, Davivienda) everywhere — withdraw in multiples of COP 100,000.
travelTips.visaFree: US, EU, UK, Canada, Australia — visa-free for 90 days (extendable to 180 days). Passport valid for 6+ months required. No yellow fever certificate needed for most areas, but recommended for Amazon and Pacific coast.
travelTips.bestOverallMonths: December to March and June to August (dry seasons). Varies by region — Caribbean coast driest Dec–Apr; Coffee Region and Andes best Jun–Sep; Amazon driest Jun–Sep.
travelTips.winterNote: Colombia has no winter — straddling the equator means consistent temperatures year-round. Altitude determines climate: Bogotá is cool (8–18°C), Medellín is spring-like (17–28°C), and the coast is hot (28–34°C).
travelTips.simCard: Buy a Claro, Movistar, or Tigo SIM at the airport for ~COP 15,000–30,000 ($4–7) with good 4G data. Coverage excellent in cities, spotty in rural areas and Amazon.
travelTips.transport: Domestic flights are cheap — book Avianca, LATAM, Viva, or Satena. Long-distance buses comfortable (look for 'ejecutivo' or 'preferencial' class). Uber works in major cities. For Coffee Region, old Willys jeeps are the local transport.
